text.title: Architecture Health Weekly Report Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Architecture Health Weekly Report Template methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Hard rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: One report per week; merging two weeks into one entry loses the delta the report is for.
rule.rationale: The report's value IS the week-over-week delta; merging hides the signal.

rule.statement: Hot-spot list is computed from data (top decile churn × top decile incident), not from architect intuition.
rule.rationale: Intuitive lists drift toward what the architect already wants to discuss; data-driven lists surface surprises.

rule.statement: Each open risk has a named owner with deadline; 'team' / 'we' as owner is rejected.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; risks decay without a named human.

rule.statement: Each weekly report names exactly one decision-needed item; zero or two+ both indicate the meeting agenda is broken.
rule.rationale: Zero decisions = report is a newsletter; two+ = the meeting will overflow and one will be skipped.

rule.statement: Debt is reported as week-over-week delta (paid down vs added), not as an absolute total.
rule.rationale: Absolute totals hide whether the team is gaining or losing ground; only deltas drive behavior.
